meta.title: Employers | Pro-Tech Staffing — Industrial & Manufacturing Staffing
meta.description: Request industrial, electronics manufacturing, and warehouse workers from Pro-Tech Staffing. Temporary staffing, temp-to-hire, direct placement, payroll services, PEO, and consulting.

hero.heading: Need workers? Let's fill the role.
hero.description: Tell us the role, shift, location, and start date. We move fast and keep you updated every step of the way.
hero.servingNote: Serving Richardson, Bedford, Austin, Tampa, San Jose, and Phoenix.

services.heading: More than temp workers.
services.description: Most employers only think of Pro-Tech as temp staffing. We offer a full range of workforce solutions built around the way industrial teams actually operate.

temporaryStaffing.title: Temporary Staffing
temporaryStaffing.desc: Fill shifts fast with pre-screened workers ready for production, warehouse, and industrial roles.

tempToHire.title: Temp-to-Hire
tempToHire.desc: Try before you commit. Evaluate workers on the job before converting them to full-time.

directPlacement.title: Direct Placement
directPlacement.desc: We recruit and place permanent hires for hard-to-fill roles so your team can focus on production.

payrollServices.title: Payroll Services
payrollServices.desc: Offload payroll administration while keeping your workforce flexible and compliant.

peo.title: PEO (Professional Employer Organization)
peo.desc: Shared HR, benefits, and compliance support for growing teams that need structure without overhead.

consulting.title: Consulting (Workforce / Technology)
consulting.desc: Strategic guidance on workforce planning, process improvement, and technology adoption for industrial operations.

certification.heading: Certified workers before they reach your floor.
certification.description1: The biggest complaint in electronics manufacturing staffing: there aren't enough qualified, certified candidates in the market. Agencies send bodies. We send trained workers.
certification.description2: Pro-Tech has certified IPC trainers on staff who train and certify candidates before they start. Your workers arrive ready to perform on day one.

ipc610.title: IPC-610
ipc610.subtitle: Acceptability of Electronic Assemblies
ipc610.desc: The industry standard for evaluating electronic assembly quality. Workers trained to identify and classify defects.

ipc620.title: IPC-620
ipc620.subtitle: Cable and Wire Harness Assemblies
ipc620.desc: Requirements and acceptance criteria for cable and wire harness assemblies used in electronics manufacturing.

jstd001.title: J-STD-001
jstd001.subtitle: Soldering
jstd001.desc: The joint industry standard for soldering. Workers certified in process control and workmanship requirements.

certification.footerHighlight: In-house certified IPC trainers on staff.
certification.footerNote: Candidates arrive certified and ready to perform on day one.

cta.heading: Ready to staff up?
cta.description: Send us the role, shift, location, and start date. Our team will respond within one business day.

form.name: Name
form.responseTime: We'll respond within one business day.

howItWorks.heading: From request to workers on-site — in three steps.

steps.title: Tell us what you need
steps.desc: Role, headcount, shift, location, and start date. No lengthy intake — a few details get us moving.

steps.title: We source and screen locally
steps.desc: Our local recruiters draw from an active, pre-vetted talent pool. Background checks, drug screening, and skills and attendance verification happen before anyone is sent.

steps.title: Workers arrive ready
steps.desc: Screened, shift-matched candidates show up on time and ready to contribute from day one. We follow up in the first week to confirm the fit.

howItWorks.testimonialQuote: Pro-Tech filled 14 positions for us in under 48 hours during peak season. More important, the workers showed up — and 12 converted to full-time. That consistency matters more than any rate card.

screening.heading: Every worker is screened before they reach your floor.
screening.intro: Agencies send bodies. We send workers who've been verified for eligibility, background, skills, attendance, and the specific demands of your shift and site. Here's exactly what every candidate goes through before placement.

steps.title: Work eligibility & E-Verify
steps.desc: I-9 verification and E-Verify on every hire. We confirm the right to work before a candidate is ever sent to your site.

steps.title: Background checks
steps.desc: Criminal background screening scaled to the role and site requirements — county, state, and national checks where appropriate.

steps.title: Drug screening
steps.desc: Pre-placement drug testing, with panel and cadence matched to safety-sensitive and DOT-regulated roles when required.

steps.title: Skills & experience verification
steps.desc: Role-specific skills are verified — hands-on or demonstrated — not taken from a resume. A machine operator is tested on the equipment; an assembler on the work.

steps.title: Attendance & reference checks
steps.desc: We verify attendance history and check references with prior employers. No-show history is the #1 predictor of whether a placement sticks, so we screen for it directly.

steps.title: Certification verification
steps.desc: We verify current certifications — forklift, OSHA, and IPC-A-610 / IPC-620 / J-STD-001 for electronics. For electronics roles, our in-house IPC trainers can certify candidates before placement.

steps.title: Shift, site & fit screening
steps.desc: We match each candidate to the actual shift, environment, and commute — so the person who shows up on day one is one who can realistically stay.

screening.closingHeading: And every worker is ours, not yours.
screening.closingBody: Each placed worker is a Pro-Tech W-2 employee. We carry the payroll, workers' compensation, and employment compliance — so the screening, the paperwork, and the liability stay on our side.
screening.ctaBody: Tell us the role and shift. We'll send candidates who've cleared every step above.
